zh.Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de St Leger於大約1086年出生在英國素可費艾利特時，他的父親William de St Leger27歲，母親Cecilia Romney23歲。他於1111年與Agnes Clare在英國英格蘭素可費艾利特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1144年在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de St Leger于大约1086年出生在英国素可費艾利特时，他的父亲William de St Leger27岁，母亲Cecilia Romney23岁。他于1111年与Agnes Clare在英国英格兰素可費艾利特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1144年在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de St Leger was born about 1086, in Fairlight, Sussex, England, his father, William de St Leger, was 27 and his mother, Cecilia Romney, was 23. He married Agnes Clare in 1111, in Fairlight, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died about 1144, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de St Leger est né environ 1086, à Fairlight, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William de St Leger, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Romney, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Clare en 1111, à Fairlight, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé environ 1144, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de St Leger um 1086 in Fairlight, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de St Leger 27 und seine Mutter Cecilia Romney 23. Er hat 1111 in Fairlight, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Agnes Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb um 1144 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de St Leger è nato il circa 1086 nel Fairlight, Sussex, England, il suo padre, William de St Leger, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia Romney, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Clare nel 1111, nel Fairlight, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì circa 1144, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de St Legerが約1086年にイギリススセックスファイルリトで生まれた時，父William de St Legerは27歳，母Cecilia Romneyは23歳であった。1111年にイギリスイギリススセックスファイルリトでAgnes Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は約1144年に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de St Leger(이)가 대략 1086년 영국 수세스 파어라이트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de St Leger(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia Romney(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1111년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 파어라이트에서 Agnes Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1144년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de St Leger nasceu aproximadamente 1086, em Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de St Leger, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Romney, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Clare em 1111, em Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1144, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de St Leger родился около 1086 г. в местности феирлаит, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William de St Leger было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Romney, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Clare в 1111 г. в местности феирлаит, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер около 1144 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de St Leger nació aproximadamente 1086, en Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, William de St Leger, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Cecilia Romney, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Agnes Clare en 1111, en Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1144, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años.
